entries.reason: CCIP send path cannot be executed against production allowlists in CI forks; mockCall isolates ENS relay correctness.
entries.replacement_recommendation: Replace with dedicated local CCIP harness once test sender allowlisting is available.

entries.reason: ENS base node ownership cannot be transferred to ephemeral test accounts on public forks; vm.store is required to exercise receiver flow.
entries.replacement_recommendation: Replace with delegated test-owned ENS test node when available.

entries.reason: Mainnet ENS receiver tests require simulated control of greengoods.eth without private-key ownership in CI.
entries.replacement_recommendation: Use a dedicated CI ENS node delegation path if introduced.

entries.reason: Arbitrum CCIP router send cannot be called from unallowlisted CI sender accounts.
entries.replacement_recommendation: Swap to real ccipSend once CI sender allowlisting or local router simulation is available.

entries.reason: Sepolia ENS tests require deterministic CCIP send behavior without external allowlist dependencies.
entries.replacement_recommendation: Replace with real router calls under a test-allowlisted sender.

entries.reason: Isolation tests validate protocol-side ENS behavior, not CCIP transport; deterministic router stub is required.
entries.replacement_recommendation: Move to integration with a local CCIP test harness.

entries.reason: Extended Arbitrum E2E flow currently cannot execute real router send from ephemeral CI sender.
entries.replacement_recommendation: Promote to real CCIP routing once sender allowlist is available in CI.

entries.reason: Extended Sepolia E2E flow stubs router send due external allowlist constraints.
entries.replacement_recommendation: Promote to real router send after test sender allowlisting.

entries.reason: Deterministic ERC20 scaffolding is used to validate account execution semantics without external token variance.
entries.replacement_recommendation: Pair with sentinel scenario using real fork token interactions.

entries.reason: Deterministic ERC20 scaffolding is used to validate account execution semantics without external token variance.
entries.replacement_recommendation: Pair with sentinel scenario using real fork token interactions.

entries.reason: MockCVStrategy provides deterministic conviction behavior while fork-backed module wiring is validated.
entries.replacement_recommendation: Add paired real-strategy sentinel test for nonlinear conviction math.

entries.reason: MockCVStrategy provides deterministic conviction behavior while fork-backed module wiring is validated.
entries.replacement_recommendation: Add paired real-strategy sentinel test for nonlinear conviction math.

entries.reason: Extended Arbitrum E2E currently uses deterministic conviction strategy scaffolding.
entries.replacement_recommendation: Pair with fork-backed real-strategy E2E sentinel scenario.

entries.reason: Fork harness still requires deterministic token scaffolding for protocol bootstrap.
entries.replacement_recommendation: Migrate bootstrap path to fork-native token fixtures where feasible.

entries.reason: CookieJar fork harness uses deterministic token scaffolding for stable withdrawal and split assertions.
entries.replacement_recommendation: Pair with sentinel scenarios using real ERC4626-vault assets where available.

entries.reason: Local E2E conviction suite remains deterministic and intentionally mock-heavy pending fork-backed parity coverage.
entries.replacement_recommendation: Pair with real-contract fork-backed conviction E2E sentinel scenario.
